title: pick_set_v1
description: B079 — the durable record of ONE human pick-of-3 (MAJORS LAW: major-occasion posts and brand-voice are never single-shot, always a human pick among 3 candidates). Distinct from the portal CARD (build_pick_item.py): the card is the UI; this is the event-grade record of what was chosen, by whom, and which ledger event it produced. Capturing Mohamed's (or the client's) pick is THE TOP — it is how the system learns the eye.

pick_set_ulid.description: ULID for this pick-set. Stable across the open→picked lifecycle.

kind.description: voice = brand-voice calibration pick (which voice is the brand's). major_post = a major-occasion post pick (MAJORS LAW).

handle.description: Client handle the pick belongs to, e.g. 'eatjurisha'. Optional for cross-client voice seeds.

slot.description: The slot this pick fills. For major_post: 'YYYY-MM-DD__occasion'. For voice: a voice-pair slug. Mirrors build_pick_item's slot.

candidates.description: Exactly 3 candidates judged together (pick-of-3). Each names its brain/source and the option key the picker chose among.

option_id.description: Stable id for this candidate within the set (e.g. the brain key 'cd_01' or 'A'/'B'/'C').

source.description: Where the candidate came from: a CD-brain key, a model, or 'client_seed'.

post_ref.description: Optional path/ref to the rendered post unit (clients/<handle>/posts/<slot>__pick_<brain>.json).

picked.description: option_id of the chosen candidate; null while status=open. MUST match one candidate's option_id when status=picked (enforced by the reader, not by JSON Schema alone).

picker.description: Who made the pick. null while open. 'mohamed' picks feed gold/taste; 'client' picks feed the client's own fingerprint.

status.description: open = awaiting a tap. picked = decided. expired = window passed unpicked. voided = pick-set retracted (e.g. diversity failure).

diversity_ok.description: False = 2+ candidates converged on the same scene/person (anti-sameness gate, build_pick_item). A converged set must not be shown for a real pick.

resulting_event_ulid.description: ULID of the client_event (type 'pick_selected') emitted when this set was picked. The Consumer-Law wire: a pick with no resulting event is an orphan.

created.description: ISO date the set was opened.

picked_at.description: ISO date-time the pick was made; null while open.
